overview: TestFlow is a minimal single-component web application built with Go's net/http that serves a server-rendered HTML page displaying a "Hello World" greeting with dynamic name personalization. The Go service handles both static page serving and form submission, returning sanitized HTML responses. No additional backend or database is needed.
requirements: The root path (/) must serve an HTML page with 'Hello World' as the primary heading
A text input form must allow users to submit their name, updating the greeting to 'Hello, {name}!'
Default greeting must be 'Hello World' when no name is provided
Page must be responsive, centered, with readable typography and proper semantic HTML
User input must be sanitized to prevent XSS attacks
Undefined routes must return a 404 error page
The application must return HTTP 200 for successful requests to /
Page must load within 2 seconds on standard broadband
All interactive elements must be keyboard-navigable and meet WCAG 2.1 Level A contrast requirements
A /health endpoint must return HTTP 200 for liveness checks

components.name: testflow-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: TestFlow Web
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Simple Hello World web application
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Serve the Hello World page
      parameters:
        - name: name
          in: query
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Optional name for personalized greeting
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with greeting
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a Go service using net/http on port 9090.

Routes:
- GET / : Serve an HTML page with an inline CSS styled greeting. Accept an optional query parameter 'name'. If provided, sanitize it using html.EscapeString() and display 'Hello, {name}!' as the h1. If absent or empty, display 'Hello World'. The page must include: a <form> with method GET and action '/', a <label> for the input, a text <input name="name">, and a submit <button>. Layout must be centered with max-width 600px, responsive, readable font (system font stack, 1.2rem+), WCAG 2.1 Level A compliant contrast (dark text on light background, e.g. #333 on #fff). Include <title>TestFlow</title>. All form elements must have proper labels and be keyboard-navigable.
- GET /health : Return JSON {"status":"ok"} with 200.
- Any other route: Return a styled 404 HTML page with appropriate status code.

Use Go's html/template for rendering. Sanitize all user input with html.EscapeString before embedding. Do not use any external dependencies — stdlib only. Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.22-alpine for build, gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 for runtime. Expose port 9090.
